title: Cyber Blackmail, Fake ID & Online Harassment Remedy in Bangladesh 2026: CID & Police Helpline Guide
metaDescription: Step-by-step emergency guide to reporting online blackmail, sextortion, fake Facebook profiles, and cyberstalking in Bangladesh — CID Cyber Crime Unit, 999 hotline, and Cyber Tribunal legal actions.
keywords: CID cyber crime unit complaint online
fake Facebook ID complaint Bangladesh police
heroIntro: With over 120 million internet users in Bangladesh, digital crime, blackmail, fake social media profiles, and financial hacking have reached alarming levels. Women, students, and professionals frequently fall victim to sextortion, non-consensual photo sharing, or defamatory Facebook pages. Under the Cyber Security Act 2023 and Penal Code 1860, law enforcement agencies in Bangladesh have specialized cyber divisions to track IP addresses, remove offensive content, and arrest perpetrators. For victim protection and Cyber Tribunal representation, consult Supreme Court Advocate Advocate Md. Shah Alam.

quickAnswer.heading: ⚡ Emergency Cyber Victim Action Plan in Bangladesh
points: Preserve Evidence: Take unedited screenshots of URL links, chats, phone numbers, and profile IDs.
Emergency Hotline: Call National Emergency Service 999 or Police Cyber Support for Women (PCSW) hotline 01769691522.
File GD / Complaint: Lodge a General Diary (GD) at nearest Police Station or submit online via CID Cyber Police portal.
Content Removal: CID / BTRC requests Facebook (Meta) / Google to block/take down abusive links.
Cyber Tribunal Suit: File a formal complaint petition before the Cyber Tribunal Court in Dhaka.

sections.h2: Common Types of Cyber Crimes in Bangladesh (Sextortion, Defamation, Hacking)
sections.content:
Digital offences in Bangladesh are prosecuted under the Cyber Security Act 2023, Pornography Control Act 2012, and the Penal Code 1860:

  • Sextortion & Intimate Photo Blackmail: Demanding money or favors under threat of leaking private photos/videos (Pornography Control Act Sec 8 — up to 7 years imprisonment).
  • Fake Facebook / WhatsApp Profiles: Creating fake accounts using someone else's name and photo to defame or harass (Cyber Security Act Sec 24 — Impersonation).
  • Cyberstalking & Harassment: Sending abusive, threatening, or sexually explicit messages repeatedly.
  • Financial Hacking & MFS Fraud: Hacking bKash/Nagad accounts, phishing, or credit card fraud.

Consult a qualified Criminal & Cyber Lawyer in Dhaka immediately upon encountering online blackmail.

If you or a family member is facing active digital blackmail, contact these specialized units:

  • National Emergency Helpline: 999 (Free toll-free call, available 24/7).
  • Police Cyber Support for Women (PCSW): Hotline: +8801769691522 / Facebook Page: Police Cyber Support for Women.
  • CID Cyber Police Centre (CPC): Hotline: +8801730336431 / Email: smr_cpc@police.gov.bd / Location: Malibagh, Dhaka.
  • BTRC Cyber Threat Detection Team: Email: btrc@btrc.gov.bd for blocking domain links in Bangladesh.

sections.h2: Step-by-step Guide: How to Preserve Evidence Legally
sections.content:
Cyber complaints fail if digital evidence is deleted or improperly captured. Follow these exact steps:

  1. Capture Full URLs: Do not just screenshot the image; copy the complete profile web link (e.g., facebook.com/profile.php?id=1000...).
  2. Uncropped Screenshots: Take full-screen screenshots showing date, time, battery bar, and phone numbers.
  3. Chat Export: Export WhatsApp/Messenger chat logs as text files.
  4. Audio / Call Recording: Save original audio call recordings without editing.
  5. Do NOT Delete: Never delete the chat thread or deactivate your account before police inspect the digital footprint.

sections.h2: Filing Complaint at CID Cyber Police Centre (Maliibagh, Dhaka)
sections.content:
For victims in Dhaka or anywhere across Bangladesh, submitting a complaint to the CID Cyber Crime Investigation Center yields rapid technical results:

  • Visit Malibagh CID Headquarters in person or file via email/online portal.
  • Submit written complaint along with evidence screenshots, GD copy, and NID.
  • CID cyber forensic experts analyze IP logs, IMEI numbers, and tower locations to identify the offender's physical location.
  • CID coordinates directly with Meta (Facebook), WhatsApp, and Google legal response teams for content takedown.

sections.h2: Filing Case before Dhaka Cyber Tribunal
sections.content:
If police delay action, victims have the legal right to file a direct Cyber Tribunal Complaint Petition before the Cyber Tribunal Court located at Dhaka Judicature:

  • Advocate files petition under relevant sections of Cyber Security Act 2023 / Pornography Control Act.
  • The Cyber Tribunal Judge examines victim statement and directs PBI (Police Bureau of Investigation) or CID to submit an investigation report within 30 days.
  • Upon report submission, the Tribunal issues arrest warrants against the offender.

sections.h2: Penalties for Fake IDs, Cyberstalking & Photo Manipulation
sections.content:
Law penalties under Bangladesh Cyber and Penal Statutes:

  • Sextortion / Publishing Intimate Photos: 2 to 7 years rigorous imprisonment + BDT 2 to 5 Lakh fine.
  • Identity Theft & Fake Profiles: Up to 5 years imprisonment + BDT 5 Lakh fine.
  • Cyber Defamation & Hacking: 3 to 5 years imprisonment.

sections.h2: Protecting Victim Privacy & Identity in Court Proceedings
sections.content:
Victims often fear social stigma when filing police reports. Under statutory cyber guidelines:

  • Female victims can present testimony in camera (private court chamber).
  • Law enforcement agencies mask victim identity and blurred evidence files in official court registers.
  • Victims have full legal protection from harassment during investigation.

sections.h2: Why You Need a Qualified Cyber Lawyer in Dhaka
sections.content:
Handling cyber harassment requires immediate legal intervention to secure stay orders, block viral links, and prosecute offenders in court.

Advocate Md. Shah Alam — Supreme Court Advocate in Dhaka — provides urgent legal protection, CID filing support, and Cyber Tribunal representation for cyber crime victims.

⚖️ Victim of Online Blackmail or Cyber Harassment? Contact Supreme Court Advocate Advocate Md. Shah Alam for immediate legal protection and Cyber Tribunal action. Visit our Criminal Legal Services Page or Contact Us Directly.

faqs.question: Can Facebook remove a fake ID or leaked photo if I report it in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: Yes. BTRC and CID Cyber Police have direct government portal access with Meta (Facebook) to expedite urgent takedowns of impersonation profiles and non-consensual intimate imagery.

faqs.question: Can police track a fake Facebook account created from a VPN?
faqs.answer: Yes. Law enforcement cyber units use advanced network forensics, mobile tower data, bKash/Nagad transactional logs, and device IMEI tracking to pinpoint the culprit regardless of basic VPN use.

faqs.question: Is cyber blackmail a bailable offence in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: No. Offences under the Pornography Control Act 2012 and major provisions of the Cyber Security Act 2023 involving blackmail and extortion are non-bailable.

faqs.question: Do I need to pay police to report a cyber crime in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: No. Reporting a cyber complaint to CID or 999 or filing a Police Station GD is completely free of charge under official government rules.
